panels.visual_description: Watercolor rendering. Professor Aronnax sitting at a wooden desk, bathed in soft, diffused light filtering through a porthole. Low Angle. Color palette is muted blues and grays, with bleeds around the edges of objects. Melancholic atmosphere.
panels.dialogue: "Let us reflect, and take our time. Hunting otters in submarine forests... will pass; but going up and down at the bottom of the sea... is quite another thing!"

panels.visual_description: Watercolor rendering. Conseil and Ned Land enter the room, their faces animated with excitement. Dutch Tilt, emphasizing their carefree attitude. Washes of bright yellows and greens contrast with the professor's somber mood. Flowing lines suggest their movement.
panels.dialogue: Faith, sir, your Captain Nemo—the devil take him!—has just made us a very pleasant offer.

panels.visual_description: Watercolor rendering. Close-up on Professor Aronnax's face, his expression a mix of surprise and concern. Soft edges and bleeds around his features convey his inner turmoil. The color scheme shifts to darker blues and purples, reflecting his anxiety. Vast empty space around his head.

Watercolor rendering. Conseil standing proudly, explaining the invitation. Camera zooms out slightly. Use of bright, transparent watercolors to depict the room, with details fading into the background. Flowing light suggests the airy nature of the situation.
panels.dialogue: If agreeable to you, sir, the commander of the _Nautilus_ has invited us to visit the magnificent Ceylon fisheries to-morrow, in your company.

panels.visual_description: Watercolor rendering. Professor Aronnax is now sitting, in lecturing position, gesturing with his hand. Ned Land and Conseil are in the foreground, in listening poses. The color scheme is light greens to show learning. Vastness surrounds the group.
panels.dialogue: Very well; sit down, my friends, and I will teach you.

panels.visual_description: Watercolor rendering. Ned Land's face, in a close up, asking a question. Use of dark reds and browns to show intensity. Flowing lines blur in the background, vast and deep.
Watercolor rendering. Professor Aronnax's face in profile, with soft colors. He appears to be speaking in a lecturing tone. Use of blues and whites in the background to show knowledge.
panels.dialogue: My worthy Ned, to the poet, a pearl is a tear of the sea; to the Orientals, it is a drop of dew solidified; to the ladies, it is a jewel...

panels.visual_description: Watercolor rendering. Conseil slightly turned, listening intently. Soft color palette of grays and blues. He seems to be absorbing knowledge.
Watercolor rendering. A wide shot of the Professor, Ned, and Conseil, sitting, the professor lecturing. The vastness of the open ocean behind them. Use of many thin lines to show depth.
panels.dialogue: Yes, mussels of certain waters in Scotland, Wales, Ireland, Saxony, Bohemia, and France.

panels.visual_description: Watercolor rendering. A close up of Ned Land with a smile. Use of dark grays and browns. The flow of the conversation is apparent.
panels.dialogue: Good! For the future I shall pay attention.

panels.visual_description: Watercolor rendering. The professor is standing and pointing. Use of bright light to suggest an idea. The lines are thin, and the colors are vast.
panels.dialogue: The pearl is nothing but a nacreous formation, deposited in a globular form, either adhering to the oyster shell, or buried in the folds of the creature.

panels.visual_description: Watercolor rendering. A close up of Conseil is staring intently. The coloring is washed out and faded.
panels.dialogue: Are many pearls found in the same oyster?

panels.visual_description: Watercolor rendering. Wide shot. The professor is now facing Ned, who looks surprised. Color washes are faint.
panels.dialogue: One oyster has been mentioned, though I allow myself to doubt it, as having contained no less than a hundred and fifty sharks.

panels.visual_description: Watercolor rendering. A close up of Ned's face. Deep colors, very bold.
Watercolor rendering. Back to a wide shot. The professor looks very nervous and sheepish. He's obviously made a mistake.
panels.dialogue: Did I say sharks? I meant to say a hundred and fifty pearls. Sharks would not be sense.

panels.visual_description: Watercolor rendering. Conseil, who is the calm one, has an inquisitive tone.
panels.dialogue: Certainly not; but will you tell us now by what means they extract these pearls?
